consolidated.summary: Margaret Elaine Forsythe's consolidated record combines 3 documents: Discharge Summary, Lab Report, GP Consultation Note. Conditions documented across the record include type 2 diabetes mellitus, hypertension, ischaemic heart disease. The highest workflow priority is high, driven by acute myocardial injury pattern. Each action remains subject to human confirmation.

medication_records.source_text: Metformin 500mg BD — NOTE: patient states she stopped this herself 'a few months ago' due to GI upset.

view of reduced EF — commenced Sacubitril-Valsartan 24/26 mg BD (Entresto) in place of ramipril. Bisoprolol

result.summary: Margaret Elaine Forsythe was reviewed from a discharge summary. Conditions documented include type 2 diabetes mellitus, hypertension. Key findings include eGFR 68, BNP: 810 pg/mL, central chest pain, diaphoresis. Documented treatment includes Metformin 500mg BD, Insulin Glargine (Lantus) 24 units at night. The case needs prompt review. A qualified professional should confirm the result.

medication_records.source_text: - Entresto (sacubitril/valsartan 24/26mg BD) — NEW, she's started it, seems ok so far,
reminded her the ramipril is STOPPED. She had some left in her pill box and had taken
- Bisoprolol 7.5mg OD — increased from 5mg. She's managing it. Pulse today 58 — acceptable.
- Furosemide 40mg OD — new addition. She's noticed she's going to the loo more in the morning.
- Aspirin 75mg + Clopidogrel 75mg — continuing both. I've prescribed the omeprazole 20mg as
- Aspirin 75mg + Clopidogrel 75mg — continuing both. I've prescribed the omeprazole 20mg as
- Aspirin 75mg + Clopidogrel 75mg — continuing both. I've prescribed the omeprazole 20mg as
- Atorvastatin 80mg nocte — continuing, no issues.
- Insulin Glargine 28 units nocte — increased by diabetic team. Blood sugars she says have been
- Ferrous Sulphate 200mg BD — new. Tolerating ok with food.

result.summary: Margaret Forsythe was reviewed from a gp consultation note. Conditions documented include CKD, post-MI. Key findings include BP: 118/72, O2 sats: 96%, breathless, Ankles still slightly puffy. Documented treatment includes Entresto (sacubitril/valsartan 24/26mg BD), ramipril. The case needs prompt review. A qualified professional should confirm the result.

overall_recommendation.headline: High-priority coordinated review
overall_recommendation.immediate_next_step: Escalate to the acute cardiac pathway
overall_recommendation.coordination_plan: Escalate for same-day medication safety review and confirm ramipril is stopped via Prescribing clinician / heart failure team; Arrange early heart failure follow-up via Heart failure service; confirm 5 missing information items before case closure; reconcile conflicting information across the record.
overall_recommendation.rationale: Possible acute cardiac concern; Acute myocardial injury pattern; additional concerns include potential ace inhibitor and entresto interaction, potentially unstable heart failure.

discrepancies.value_b: Iodinated contrast allergy | rash, mild, resolving spontaneously
discrepancies.action_required: Verify the original reaction and correct the allergy record before future contrast imaging.
Confirm ramipril is stopped and the remaining supply is removed.
Use the latest renal function for medication dosing and follow-up.

safety_warnings: Acute myocardial injury pattern: Escalate to the acute cardiac pathway
Potential ACE inhibitor and Entresto interaction: Escalate for same-day medication safety review and confirm ramipril is stopped
Potentially unstable heart failure: Arrange early heart failure follow-up
Poor glycaemic control: Route to the diabetes nurse specialist within 7 days
Incorrect allergy severity may be recorded: Verify the original contrast reaction and correct the allergy record
